rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF AKIKO SPINDLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/10/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Proof of payment of the assessment fee must be filed before an accounting can be approved. (2) The amended accounting petition was e-filed on 7/24/24, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. (3) Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (4) Item 2 of the proof of service e-filed on 8/1/24 is incomplete. (5) Income tax returns have reportedly been prepared. Were they filed? (6) Page 2 of Schedule E appears to be missing. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/29/24. Not all of this amended accounting petition has been reviewed, as a courtesy copy of the related third amended trust accounting still has not been provided to the examiner. The distributions to the trust (Schedule K) does not appear to agree with the assets received by trustee Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (It is not necessary to file a "Declaration" to address issues; the amended accounting petition and actions, such as filing the proof of payment of the assessment fee, would have been sufficient without answering issues in an numbered "declaration." There appears to be a page missing from the "Declaration" e-filed on 7/31/24. It is also unclear what the copy of the Merrill Lynch statement attached to the "Declaration" is intended to support. Please do NOT file another declaration to address these issues. The amended accounting petition must just be supplemented or amended by the conservator to clarify the status of income tax returns and provide the missing page 2 of Schedule E. [CRC 7.3.] A new proof of service must be filed, showing that notice of the First Amended Fourth Account, etc., was given at least 15 days before 9/10/24. Proof of payment of the assessment fee must be filed.)

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF THOMAS FRANCISCO LEGASPI FERIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The accounting was filed by the petitioners presumably as limited co-conservators of the estate, but neither the appointment order nor the underlying petition specified any powers or duties applicable to the limited co-conservators of the estate. See Probate Code sections 1821(j)(1)(A) & 1830(b). As such, the petitioners appear to have no authority in their capacity as limited co-conservators of the estate to manage the limited conservatee's Chase bank account (which has been inventoried and appraised as the sole asset of this limited conservatorship estate) or any other finance for that matter, although the Chase account statements attached to the accounting indicate that the limited conservatee and petitioner Lorna Feria continue to be joint account holders, allowing Lorna to manage the account in her individual capacity (as a joint owner). The Court is to determine whether to correct its appointment order on its own motion to remove the limited conservatorship of the estate as a clerical error, and if so, whether to take this accounting petition off calendar, as no court accountings are required unless and until a limited conservatorship of the estate is properly established. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY K. MORITZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/18/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A Court-appointed fiduciary must supplement or amend her/his/their own pleading(s), not the attorney. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] (2) Decedent's brother, John Moritz, was not listed in item 8 of the initial Petition for Letters of Administration. When did he die? Was he survived by issue? (3) No new proof of service has been filed yet, showing that notice was mailed with a printout of the procedures for how to make a remote appearance attached to it. (4) The separately mailed Notice of Directions for Remote Appearance and Access filed on 5/2/24 showed a different address for Robert J. Moritz from the one reported in the petition to be his, and his address changed again on the proof of service of the attorney's "supplemental declaration" filed on 5/3/24. This has not yet been addressed. (5) If notice to the Franchise Tax Board was only mailed on 6/18/24, the estate was not yet ready to close. (6) If there were 4,433.33 shares of Marathon Oil Corp. on hand, each heir should receive 886.666 shares, not 866.666, as shown in the attorney's declaration filed on 7/15/24. Deadline is 9/9/24 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of the complete proof of service and the administrator's verified supplement addressing these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GARY WOLLIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The case-opening petition requested an order admitting the decedent's will to probate. However, the amended petition requests the Court's finding that the decedent died intestate, despite his original will on file. 3 witnesses appear to have signed the decedent's will in 1992. The will is not self-proving under Probate Code section 8220(b) because the purported attestation clause included in the will failed to qualify as an affidavit under CCP section 2015.5 (witnesses did not sign "under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of California"). The petitioner cannot locate any subscribing witness. Section 8221 provides that: "If no subscribing witness is available as a witness within the meaning of Section 240 of the Evidence Code, the court may, if the will on its face conforms to all requirements of law, permit proof of the will by proof of the handwriting of the testator and one of the following: (a) Proof of the handwriting of any one subscribing witness. (b) Receipt in evidence of one of the following documents reciting facts showing due execution of the will: (1) A writing in the will bearing the signatures of all subscribing witnesses. (2) An affidavit of a person with personal knowledge of the circumstances of the execution." PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
